description: The crate-level doc example in secretx-signature/src/lib.rs uses the ```rust,ignore fence, which means rustdoc skips compilation entirely and the example is never type-checked. Every sibling crate (secretx-aws-kms, secretx-local-signing, secretx-env) uses ```rust,no_run, which compiles the example but does not run it — catching API regressions at CI time. What goes wrong: if the Ed25519Signer::new signature changes (e.g., error type, argument type), the doc example silently rots and misleads users. The example is wrappable with `# async fn example() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> { ... }` and the backend constructed from a stub, making no_run viable. Fix: switch to rust,no_run and add the required scaffolding.
title: Doc example uses rust,ignore instead of rust,no_run
